_meta.verificationNote: AI Generated: Customer information risk assessment checklist. Organisations should adapt scope and depth proportionate to their size, complexity, and data holdings per clause 10.4.

title: Customer Information Risk Assessment Checklist
description: Structured checklist for conducting customer information risk assessments under BNM MCIPD, covering all key risk domains.
instructions: For each checklist item, assess the current state, identify gaps, assign a risk rating, and document remediation actions where applicable. The checklist should be completed at least annually or upon material changes to the operating environment.

items.question: Has the Board set and communicated a clear tone-at-the-top on the importance of safeguarding customer information?
items.evidenceExpected: Board communications, charter provisions, corporate values documentation

items.question: Has the Board formally approved the FSP's written policies on customer information protection?
items.evidenceExpected: Board minutes, resolution records, policy approval documentation

items.question: Does the Board regularly review senior management reports on customer information management including risk assessments, findings, and mitigation plans?
items.evidenceExpected: Board meeting minutes, management reports, risk assessment presentations

items.question: Does the Board obtain annual written assurance from senior management on the effectiveness of controls and OSP compliance?
items.evidenceExpected: Annual assurance letter, supporting evidence, Board acknowledgement

items.question: Are reports and mitigation plans periodically reviewed to reflect changes in the operating environment and evolving risks?
items.evidenceExpected: Review records, updated risk assessments, amended mitigation plans

items.question: Has the FSP identified and addressed potential threats and vulnerabilities to customer information (internal, external, negligent, deliberate)?
items.evidenceExpected: Threat register, vulnerability assessment reports, remediation records

items.question: Has a likelihood and impact assessment been conducted for identified threats and vulnerabilities?
items.evidenceExpected: Risk assessment documentation, likelihood-impact matrices, risk ratings

items.question: Does the risk assessment include third-party service providers and outsourcing arrangements?
items.evidenceExpected: Third-party risk assessments, OSP risk registers

items.question: Are mitigation plans developed and implemented based on identified threats?
items.evidenceExpected: Mitigation plans with owners and deadlines, implementation evidence

items.question: Is the risk assessment proportionate to the FSP's size, nature, complexity, and volume/sensitivity of customer information?
items.evidenceExpected: Proportionality rationale documentation, calibration records

items.question: Are written policies and procedures in place covering the full customer information lifecycle (collection, access, storage, use, transmission, sharing, disclosure, disposal)?
items.evidenceExpected: Policy documents covering each lifecycle stage, procedures manuals

items.question: Are policies proportionate to the FSP's size, complexity, and data sensitivity?
items.evidenceExpected: Proportionality assessment, policy scope documentation

items.question: Do specific policies exist for: (a) off-site work, (b) physical document transport, (c) portable IT equipment, (d) breach incident handling?
items.evidenceExpected: Specific policy documents for each area

items.question: Are policies readily accessible and clearly communicated to staff by the designated officer?
items.evidenceExpected: Policy portal, communication records, staff acknowledgements

items.question: Are policies continually reviewed for adequacy, relevance, and effectiveness?
items.evidenceExpected: Review records, amendment history, review schedule

items.question: Are preventive and detective ICT controls deployed and regularly monitored for effectiveness?
items.evidenceExpected: Control inventory, SIEM logs, monitoring reports, effectiveness reviews

items.question: Are appropriate controls in place for off-site work arrangements including MFA, encryption, and secure transmission?
items.evidenceExpected: Remote access architecture, MFA enforcement evidence, encryption policies

items.question: Are portable storage device downloads restricted to staff with legitimate business need, with encryption and password protection enforced?
items.evidenceExpected: Device whitelisting policy, download approval records, encryption enforcement

items.question: Is access to call recordings containing customer information restricted on a need-to-know basis?
items.evidenceExpected: Call recording access controls, audit logs, access review records

items.question: Are deterrent mechanisms in place against unauthorised disclosure (CCTV, device restrictions, disciplinary awareness, whistleblowing)?
items.evidenceExpected: CCTV coverage map, device restriction policy, disciplinary matrix, whistleblowing channel

items.question: Is access to web-based communication and social media platforms restricted for staff handling customer information?
items.evidenceExpected: Web filtering configuration, policy exceptions register, enforcement reports

items.question: Are detection mechanisms in place for unauthorised access, unusual viewing, suspicious downloads, and unauthorised disclosure?
items.evidenceExpected: SIEM use cases, alert configurations, audit trail review schedule, sample check records

items.question: Do role profiles include descriptions of access rights to customer information?
items.evidenceExpected: Role profile documents, access rights matrices, HR-IT mapping

items.question: Are customer information locations mapped across systems with access controls at application, database, OS, and network levels?
items.evidenceExpected: Data inventory, access control documentation at each level, testing evidence

items.question: Are access rights regularly reviewed and immediately revoked for departing staff or role changes?
items.evidenceExpected: Access review reports, revocation records, JML process documentation

items.question: Are physical security controls adequate for protecting customer information in paper and electronic forms?
items.evidenceExpected: Physical security assessment, access control systems, CCTV coverage

items.question: Are restricted areas (server rooms, filing rooms) access-controlled with intruder deterrents?
items.evidenceExpected: Restricted area register, access logs, intrusion detection records

items.question: Is a clear-desk policy implemented and monitored?
items.evidenceExpected: Clear-desk policy, spot check records, compliance reports

items.question: Are adequate controls in place for off-site customer information collection and data-in-transit?
items.evidenceExpected: Off-site handling procedures, secure transport evidence, device protection records

items.question: Are procedures in place to identify customer information no longer required operationally or legally?
items.evidenceExpected: Data retention schedule, identification procedures, disposal approval records

items.question: Are industry-standard secure destruction methods used (data wiping, physical destruction, degaussing)?
items.evidenceExpected: Destruction certificates, tool certifications, chain of custody records

items.question: If outsourced destruction is used, has a risk-benefit assessment been conducted?
items.evidenceExpected: Risk-benefit assessment documentation, provider evaluation records

items.question: Is customer information shredded or stored in tamper-proof containers before outsourced collection?
items.evidenceExpected: Pre-collection storage evidence, tamper-proof container procurement records

items.question: Are random checks conducted on outsourced destruction processes?
items.evidenceExpected: Random check reports, inspection records, findings and actions

items.question: Do employment contracts contain confidentiality undertakings specifying legal obligations and consequences?
items.evidenceExpected: Contract template, signed undertakings, legal obligation references

items.question: Are external vendor personnel appropriately vetted and monitored?
items.evidenceExpected: Vendor contracts with vetting requirements, verification records, monitoring reports

items.question: Is a high degree of staff awareness maintained on confidentiality, compliance, and breach consequences?
items.evidenceExpected: Awareness programme records, communication materials, acknowledgement tracking

items.question: Are robust monitoring mechanisms in place to ensure staff adhere to policies, procedures, and controls?
items.evidenceExpected: Monitoring framework, compliance reports, non-compliance escalation records

items.question: Is relevant training provided regularly, including specific onboarding training for new staff?
items.evidenceExpected: Training curriculum, completion records, onboarding programme content

items.question: Are mechanisms in place to gauge training effectiveness (e.g., annual awareness survey)?
items.evidenceExpected: Survey results, assessment scores, gap analysis, improvement actions

items.question: Are staff breaches thoroughly investigated with appropriate disciplinary actions and Board reporting?
items.evidenceExpected: Investigation records, disciplinary action records, Board reports

items.question: Has an independent review of customer information protection been conducted within the last two years?
items.evidenceExpected: Independent review report, review schedule, scope documentation

items.question: Does the review assess senior management effectiveness and control adequacy?
items.evidenceExpected: Review scope covering management effectiveness and control adequacy sections

items.question: Have written findings been communicated to senior management and the Board?
items.evidenceExpected: Written findings report, senior management and Board presentation records

items.question: Have timely corrective actions been taken to address identified deficiencies?
items.evidenceExpected: Corrective action plans, implementation evidence, closure records

items.question: Is a customer information breach response plan in place covering both deliberate and non-deliberate breaches?
items.evidenceExpected: Breach response plan document, escalation matrix, responsibility assignments

items.question: Do all staff understand escalation procedures, and are relevant staff trained on remedial actions?
items.evidenceExpected: Training records, escalation procedure communications, drill participation records

items.question: Are breach detection mechanisms in place, including customer complaint integration?
items.evidenceExpected: Detection system documentation, complaint-to-breach linkage process

items.question: Can breaches be contained immediately with appropriate mitigating actions?
items.evidenceExpected: Containment playbooks, response time metrics, cross-FSP notification procedures

items.question: Is there a structured process for BNM notification with prescribed minimum content?
items.evidenceExpected: Notification templates, trigger assessment criteria, submission records

items.question: Are investigations completed within three months with Board reporting and BNM submission?
items.evidenceExpected: Investigation timeline records, Board reports, BNM submission records

items.question: Is a breach register maintained with root causes, remedial actions, and lessons learnt?
items.evidenceExpected: Breach register, trend analysis reports

items.question: Are investigation reports retained for at least seven years from Board tabling date?
items.evidenceExpected: Retention schedule, archived reports, retention compliance records

items.question: Is there a process for notifying affected customers directly in plain language with prescribed content?
items.evidenceExpected: Customer notification templates, communication procedures, staff training records

items.question: Are OSP risks monitored on an ongoing basis?
items.evidenceExpected: OSP risk register, monitoring reports, escalation records

items.question: Is adequate due diligence performed when selecting OSPs with customer information access?
items.evidenceExpected: Due diligence assessment reports, selection criteria documentation

items.question: Do OSPs maintain controls comparable to the FSP's own standards?
items.evidenceExpected: Control assessment reports, comparable standard documentation, gap analysis

items.question: Do SLAs include all mandatory provisions per paragraph 12.4 (safeguarding, vetting, access restriction, breach reporting, data return/destruction, audit rights)?
items.evidenceExpected: SLA review checklist, contract analysis, amendment records for existing SLAs

items.question: Have binding NDAs been executed with all OSPs handling customer information?
items.evidenceExpected: NDA register, executed NDA copies, renewal tracking

items.question: Do OSPs conduct regular training for their personnel on customer information handling?
items.evidenceExpected: OSP training records, effectiveness review reports

items.question: Are OSPs reviewed at least biennially for contractual compliance?
items.evidenceExpected: OSP review reports, review schedule, findings and remediation tracking

items.question: Are accurate and complete records maintained of all customer information shared with OSPs?
items.evidenceExpected: Data sharing register, audit trail records, reconciliation reports

items.question: Is valid informed consent obtained before disclosing customer information where required?
items.evidenceExpected: Consent management records, consent forms, withdrawal mechanisms

items.question: Are cross-border disclosures governed by adequate safeguards and regulatory approvals?
items.evidenceExpected: Cross-border transfer register, adequacy assessments, regulatory approval records

items.question: Are comprehensive records maintained of all permitted disclosures with legal basis and approval evidence?
items.evidenceExpected: Disclosure register, legal basis documentation, approval records
